title: Northstar Bridge signature validation exploit
summary: Outbound transfers started failing after a verifier hotfix, while two suspicious withdrawals were flagged by monitoring.

workstreams.outcome: Classified as CRITICAL based on user disruption, funds at risk, and the incident profile. First objective is containment before message expansion.

Initial forensic posture centers on A verifier contract update appears to have accepted malformed signatures under a narrow edge case. Contract-level review should prioritize signer validation and any rollback path.
Drafted three message layers so the team does not improvise under pressure.
Converted the incident into owned work instead of an unstructured coordination thread.
Started the closure document early so the team captures facts while they are still fresh.

messages.public: We are investigating a critical incident affecting Northstar Bridge on Base. Current impact: Bridge withdrawals are paused and some pending transfers may be delayed for several hours. As a precaution, affected flows have been paused while the team validates system integrity. Next update within 15 minutes.
messages.partners: Northstar Bridge incident classification: CRITICAL. Affected contracts: BridgeVerifierV2, OutboundMessageRouter, EmergencyPauseModule. Please halt dependent automation until we confirm the signer path is safe.
messages.internal: Severity: CRITICAL (11/12) | Funds at risk: $2,400,000 | Comms discipline: one canonical timeline, one status owner, one evidence owner.

actions.task: declare incident channel, freeze unrelated deploys, confirm severity
diff verifier logic, reconcile suspicious transfers, preserve traces
publish initial user-safe update and prepare partner briefing
validate rollback or patched pause path before restoration
decide whether to extend pause window and engage external responders

postmortem.executiveSummary: Northstar Bridge experienced a critical signature validation exploit. User impact centered on bridge withdrawals are paused and some pending transfers may be delayed for several hours.
timeline: 2026-03-17T09:12:00Z incident detected via OpenZeppelin Defender alert
T+05m containment discussion opened and on-call owners assigned
T+15m first user-safe status update prepared
T+30m signer and contract diff review underway
T+60m restoration or longer pause decision prepared for leadership
prevention: add deployment diff checks for signer quorum changes
require simulated malformed-signature tests before verifier upgrades
create a pre-approved incident comms template for bridge pauses
attach post-incident runbooks to the next production readiness review
